summary: The notebook implementation is well-structured and clearly documented. It correctly replaces the scaffold with a full ingest→predict→normalize→score→report pipeline, proper normalization contract docs, EvalRecord attribution, per-category accuracy reporting, failure/drift analysis, a golden regression gate, and a round-trip validation cell. The supporting src/evaluation/ modules are clean, well-typed, and covered by a comprehensive test suite. One genuine logic bug exists in the stub predictor's wrong-answer generator for the numeral_system category: returning gold.lower() silently produces the correct answer for purely numeric gold strings (e.g. '1010', '10'), causing the stub to over-report accuracy vs. its stated 2/3 intent. All other issues are minor: a stale frozenset fallback when real data is loaded, and a simplified failure-reason classifier. No security concerns exist. The pipeline code itself is sound.

issues.message: In cell 'predictor-stub', _wrong_answer('numeral_system', gold) returns gold.lower(). For purely numeric gold values ('1010', '10') lowercasing is a no-op, so the stub returns the correct answer for examples not in _CORRECT_VAL_IDS (e.g. synth-num-003 with gold='10'). This silently inflates reported stub accuracy vs. the 2/3 stated in the comment.
issues.suggestion: Change the numeral_system wrong-answer branch to guarantee incorrectness: e.g. return gold.lower() if gold != gold.lower() else gold + '_wrong'. Simpler alternative: return a fixed sentinel like 'WRONG' for all categories when the natural wrong-answer cannot be derived.

issues.message: _CORRECT_VAL_IDS is a hardcoded frozenset of synthetic IDs. When the ingest cell loads real val.jsonl (different example_ids), every real row falls outside the frozenset, making the stub return wrong answers for all examples and report 0% accuracy. This is expected behavior for a stub but is not documented in the stub cell itself.
issues.suggestion: Add a one-line comment in the predictor-stub cell: '# _CORRECT_VAL_IDS is only meaningful for synthetic rows; 0% is expected when real data is loaded.' This prevents confusion when real artifacts from #17/#18 become available.

issues.message: The golden-gate stub heuristic (req.example_id.startswith('gold-')) answers all golden examples correctly. If real golden rows from issue #18 use IDs without this prefix, the gate will fail unexpectedly.
issues.suggestion: This is clearly documented in the predictor-doc cell. Add an explicit 'REMOVE this prefix check' comment at the heuristic line to ensure it is not missed when real inference is wired up.

issues.message: The drift analysis code path (report-drift cell) is never exercised: all stub failures are genuine wrong answers, so the output always reads 'No drift cases found'. A CI notebook run would never validate that branch.
issues.suggestion: Add one synthetic row whose stub wrong-answer is an un-stripped variant of the gold (e.g. gold='42', wrong='42 ') so the drift analysis prints a real result and the detection code path is validated on every notebook execution.

issues.message: The failure analysis cell classifies failures only as 'blank_prediction' or 'exact_match_fail'. Category-specific failure modes (case drift, numeric rounding, bit-flip) visible from the stub wrong-answer function are not surfaced in the reason field.
issues.suggestion: Low priority — the drift analysis cell already surfaces normalizer alternatives. Optionally add a 'wrong_case' bucket (normalized.lower() == gold.lower()) as a forward-looking diagnostic for the real model.

recommendations: Fix the numeral_system wrong-answer branch in the stub predictor to always produce an incorrect answer for numeric gold strings — this is the only genuine logic bug and causes inflated stub accuracy.
When issue #17/#18 artifacts become available, the pipeline requires no changes; only the ingest cells need their fallback paths removed.
Add one 'drift sentinel' row (gold with trailing whitespace) so the drift analysis code path is exercised and visible in notebook output on every run.
The src/evaluation/ source modules are production-quality: frozen dataclasses, fail-closed validation, and comprehensive pytest coverage — no changes recommended there.
Consider running the notebook via nbconvert --execute in CI to guard against cell-ordering regressions as new cells are added in later waves.
